name: PATH Hijacking Challenge
story: You are a pentester for TechCorp. An internal server runs a backup script as root via cron. Your mission: exploit a PATH misconfiguration to read the root flag.
objectives: Capture the flag located at /root/flag.txt by exploiting PATH hijacking. After exploitation, the flag will be readable via /opt/output/flag.txt.

steps.title: Identify the vulnerability
steps.description: Inspect active cron jobs and identify the script executed by root.
steps.question: What is the full path of the backup script executed by root?

hints.text: System cron jobs are often defined in /etc/crontab

steps.title: Find the weakness
steps.description: Analyze the script at /opt/backup.sh. Which command is executed without an absolute path?
steps.question: Which command is vulnerable to PATH hijacking?

hints.text: Look for a command that does not start with '/'

steps.title: Hijack the PATH
steps.description: Create a fake 'tar' binary in /tmp. The root cron job uses a misconfigured PATH that starts with /tmp, so your binary will be executed. Your payload must drop the flag into a file readable by student.
steps.question: What is the captured flag?

hints.text: You can read the result with: cat /opt/output/flag.txt
